context.description: Opening philosophical reflection that frames the book’s purpose as leading the reader toward a simple but elusive question about what keeps us from acting. The author says the book will focus on raising that question and on dispelling doubt that the described unusual things are possible.
normalization_notes: Source is a German book page with reflective introductory prose.
No chapter heading or title is visible in the provided text.

rows.text_content: Introductory reflection; transition into the main content.
rows.paragraph_text: The book begins by posing a question about what keeps us from simply doing what lies before us. It states that the book will mainly focus on making that question arise in the reader, while arguing that the strange things discussed here are indeed possible.

content.free_text: book_page nonfiction_philosophy consciousness_essay German book page 5. Introductory reflective prose about consciousness, possibility, and the question of what prevents action.
